allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Transform global graph explorer into ER diagram with user selection

Completely redesign the global graph explorer (/orgs/{orgId}/graph) to match
the Salesforce Schema Builder experience with ER-style visualization:

Features:
- Blank default state: Beautiful empty state until user is selected
- User search & selection: Search bar to find and select any user
- Clean initial view: Shows only user → profiles → permission sets → roles
- Object filter panel: Right sidebar to progressively add objects
- ER-style cards: Objects displayed as schema cards with fields inside
- Field permissions inline: Read/Edit indicators next to each field
- Dynamic node management: Smooth transitions when adding/removing objects
- Object relationships: Pink dashed lines between related objects
- Detail panel: Switches to node details when clicking nodes

User Experience:
1. Page loads blank with instructional card
2. Search for and select a user
3. Graph shows user''s profiles, permission sets, and roles
4. Use object filter panel to add specific objects
5. Objects appear as ER cards with their fields
6. Click nodes to see details, click away to return to filter panel
7. Clear button to start over with a different user

Layout:
- 8-column main graph area
- 4-column right sidebar (filter panel or details)
- Legend below graph
- User search at top

This creates a unified ER diagram experience across both the user detail
page and the global graph explorer, matching the requested Salesforce
Schema Builder functionality where you progressively build the graph
by selecting users and objects.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "fix: Prevent edge creation errors for non-existent object nodes

Fixed two issues in ERGraphVisualization:

1. Edge creation error: Edges were being added to non-existent object nodes
   during initialization. Changed logic to only add edges between base
   nodes (user, profile, permission_set, role). Object edges are added
   dynamically when objects are selected.

2. Wheel sensitivity warning: Removed custom wheelSensitivity setting
   to use Cytoscape default and avoid console warnings.

Error was: ''Can not create edge with nonexistent target object_XXX''
This occurred when permission set granted access to an object, but the
object node wasn''t in the initial graph (since objects are added on-demand).

Fix ensures clean console output and proper graph initialization.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>")
